headline: Samsung's 55-inch Frame art TV is $200 cheaper than usual
summary: Samsung's 55-inch Frame TV is currently $200 off, making it a more accessible art-displaying television. This unique 4K TV transforms into a gallery-like art piece when not in active use. Its framed design and matte finish help it seamlessly integrate into home decor.

headline: Netflix says around 300 titles used generative AI
summary: Netflix disclosed that approximately 300 titles on its platform utilized generative AI, primarily in post-production. The streaming giant aims to leverage AI to deliver higher quality content more quickly and at a lower cost. This news was shared in their second-quarter earnings report.

headline: Why are people buying so many CDs?
summary: CD sales rose by 16% year-over-year in the first half of 2026 in the US, totaling 16.3 million units. Fans are increasingly purchasing CDs as an affordable method to directly support their favorite artists. This resurgence highlights a growing interest in physical media.

headline: Kalshi says it caught Trump's teleprompter operator insider trading
summary: Prediction market platform Kalshi claims Gabriel Perez, former Trump teleprompter operator, engaged in insider trading. Perez allegedly used advance knowledge of Trump's speech content to place bets on political outcomes. Federal investigators are now probing these claims of illicit betting since 2016.

headline: GameStop CEO: Physical Discs 'Don't Matter' to Business
summary: GameStop CEO Ryan Cohen stated that physical game sales are 'totally irrelevant' to the company's overall business. His remarks have sparked significant debate within the gaming community about the future of physical media. Cohen's comments suggest a strategic shift in GameStop's focus away from traditional game sales.

headline: Pro Jank Footy Release Date Confirmed, Arrives August
summary: The highly anticipated, insanity-fueled 'Pro Jank Footy' is confirmed for an August release. This NBA Jam-inspired take on Australian Rules Football will launch in less than a month. Fans can prepare for a unique and exaggerated sports gaming experience.

headline: Save 35% Off Apple AirPods 4 Earbuds
summary: The Apple AirPods 4 earbuds with Active Noise Cancellation are currently available for just $119. This deal represents a significant 35% discount on the renewed product. Buyers receive a full 1-year Amazon warranty with their purchase.

headline: Official Street Fighter 6 Fragrances Released
summary: Capcom has collaborated with Gaming Fragrances to release official scents for Street Fighter 6 characters. Fans can now purchase perfumes designed to evoke Juri, Ed, and Ingrid. This unique partnership allows gamers to experience their favorite fighters in a new, aromatic way.

headline: Sadiq Khan Among 26 New Peers Entering the Lords
summary: London Mayor Sadiq Khan is among 26 new individuals appointed to the House of Lords. Broadcaster June Sarpong and former union chief Christina McAnea will also join as peers. These appointments are set to bring fresh perspectives to the UK's legislative body.

headline: Fifa 'Assessing Match Reports' Over Falklands Banner
summary: FIFA is reviewing match reports concerning Argentina players displaying a banner in support of their Falkland Islands claims. The football governing body will decide on potential disciplinary action against the team. The incident highlights the ongoing political sensitivity surrounding the islands.

headline: Why You Should Care About the World Cup Third-Place Play-Off
summary: Despite potential fatigue, the World Cup's third-place play-off between England and France holds significant importance. The match offers a final chance for national pride and a strong finish to the tournament. It remains an opportunity to witness elite football and celebrate the conclusion of the World Cup.

headline: Couples Free to Marry at Beach or Pub in England and Wales
summary: New government plans will grant couples in England and Wales more freedom in choosing their wedding venues. Outdated marriage laws are being updated to allow ceremonies in non-traditional locations, including beaches and pubs. These changes aim to provide greater choice and flexibility for future marriages.
